tool_args.label: 2018 MTA and TBTA Bond Refunding Policy

payload.project_name: 2018 MTA and TBTA Bond Refunding Policy
payload.description: Original Bond and Other Debt Obligations Refunding Policy adopted by the Board in December 2018, being replaced by the updated Refunding Policy

evidence_refs.source_quote: the Bond and Other Debt Obligations Refunding Policy adopted by the Board in December 2018 (the "2018 Policy")
The 2018 Policy establishes debt service savings criteria that must be met by proposed refundings of outstanding MTA and TBTA bonds.

tool_args.label: Current refunding positive NPV savings aggregate requirement

payload.claim_text: The updated Refunding Policy amends the positive net present value savings requirements for refundings of fixed rate bonds that are within 90 days of an optional call or maturity ("current refunding transactions")
payload.statement: Current refunding transactions of outstanding MTA and TBTA fixed rate bonds are permitted if the refunding transaction achieves positive net present value (NPV) savings in the aggregate
payload.existing: The 2018 Policy establishes debt service savings criteria that must be met by proposed refundings of outstanding MTA and TBTA bonds.
payload.proposed: current refunding transactions of outstanding MTA and TBTA fixed rate bonds are permitted if the refunding transaction achieves positive net present value (NPV) savings in the aggregate, while providing the latitude to structure savings patterns within a refunding transaction by permitting individual maturities of bonds to be refunded without regard to savings on an individual bond basis.

evidence_refs.source_quote: clarify that current refunding transactions of outstanding MTA and TBTA fixed rate bonds are permitted if the refunding transaction achieves positive net present value (NPV) savings in the aggregate

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Short/medium-term bond refunding at CFO discretion

payload.claim_text: The updated Refunding Policy will allow refundings of such short/medium-term fixed rate bonds at the discretion of the CFO or CFO designees notwithstanding the debt service savings criteria applicable to current or advance refundings.
payload.statement: The updated Refunding Policy will allow refundings of such short/medium-term fixed rate bonds at the discretion of the CFO or CFO designees notwithstanding the debt service savings criteria

evidence_refs.source_quote: The updated Refunding Policy will allow refundings of such short/medium-term fixed rate bonds at the discretion of the CFO or CFO designees

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Updated policy applies to 2024 refundings

payload.claim_text: The updated Refunding Policy will apply to 2024 refundings authorized by the Multiple Series Refunding Bond Supplemental Resolutions, which were approved by the Board in December 2023, to be executed after the approval of the Refunding Policy and to refundings authorized in future years until subsequent amendment, modification or repeal of the Refunding Policy.
payload.statement: The updated Refunding Policy will apply to 2024 refundings and to refundings authorized in future years
payload.subject: Updated MTA and TBTA Bond Refunding Policy

evidence_refs.source_quote: The updated Refunding Policy will apply to 2024 refundings authorized by the Multiple Series Refunding Bond Supplemental Resolutions

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Advance refunding 3.0% NPV savings threshold

payload.claim_text: An advance refunding that complies with applicable law is permitted if, at the time of final pricing, (a) the aggregate NPV savings is at least 3.0% of the par amount of the refunded bonds, and (b) the refunding of each bond to be called prior to its scheduled maturity achieves NPV savings (expressed as a percentage of the par amount of such refunded bond) of at least the threshold amounts in the table.
payload.statement: An advance refunding is permitted if aggregate NPV savings is at least 3.0% of the par amount of the refunded bonds
payload.subject: MTA and TBTA Bond and Other Debt Obligations Refunding Policy

evidence_refs.source_quote: An advance refunding that complies with applicable law is permitted if, at the time of final pricing, (a) the aggregate NPV savings is at least 3.0% of the par amount of the refunded bonds

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Policy provides enhanced flexibility

payload.claim_text: Staff believes that the updated Refunding Policy will provide enhanced flexibility in the pursuit of MTA's and TBTA's capital markets objectives in funding the capital program at the lowest possible cost.
payload.statement: Staff believes the updated Refunding Policy will provide enhanced flexibility in funding the capital program at the lowest possible cost
payload.subject: Updated MTA and TBTA Bond Refunding Policy

evidence_refs.source_quote: Staff believes that the updated Refunding Policy will provide enhanced flexibility in the pursuit of MTA's and TBTA's capital markets objectives in funding the capital program at the lowest possible cost.

warnings: claim.statement is accepted as passthrough, but prefer claim_text. Claim statement.

tool_args.label: Balloon bonds refunding exempt from savings criteria

payload.claim_text: This policy does not restrict the refunding of fixed rate bonds for which principal installments are deemed to amortize over a period of time based on substantially level debt service as estimated by the issuer of the fixed rate bonds for purposes of determining calculated debt service as required by the applicable bond resolution (Balloon Bonds).
payload.statement: Balloon Bonds refundings are not restricted by this policy and may be undertaken if the CFO determines it is in the best interest of the MTA
payload.subject: MTA and TBTA Bond and Other Debt Obligations Refunding Policy

evidence_refs.source_quote: the refunding of fixed rate bonds for which principal installments are deemed to amortize over a period of time based on substantially level debt service as estimated by the issuer of the fixed rate bonds for purposes of determining calculated debt service as required by the applicable bond resolution (Balloon Bonds)

tool_args.label: Board cycle timing risk

payload.claim_text: The Board could determine that the 2018 Policy remain in effect without change, requiring staff to seek approval for specific refundings that cannot be structured within the existing 2018 Policy. This alternative is not advised as the timing of the Board cycle could result in missed market opportunities.
payload.statement: Keeping the 2018 Policy without change is not advised as the timing of the Board cycle could result in missed market opportunities
payload.subject: MTA and TBTA Bond Refunding Policy
